verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) cancer/tumor cells display HSP60 on the cell surface as a stress-induced ecto-chaperone, making it a target for cancer immunotherapy and ADC approaches; (2) heat shock, oxidative stress, and other stress states induce its PM surface exposure on various cell types; (3) apoptosis and ICD also result in PM-surface HSP60 display. These documented ecto-pools qualify as cell_state_induced contextual surface biology.
